solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int numberOfSpecialChars(String word) {
        int[] firstUpper = new int[26];
        int[] firstLower = new int[26];
        Arrays.fill(firstUpper, Integer.MAX_VALUE);
        Arrays.fill(firstLower, Integer.MAX_VALUE);

        for (int i = 0; i < word.length(); i++) {
            char c = word.charAt(i);
            if (Character.isLowerCase(c)) {
                int idx = c - 'a';
                if (firstLower[idx] == Integer.MAX_VALUE) {
                    firstLower[idx] = i;
                }
            } else {
                int idx = c - 'A';
                if (firstUpper[idx] == Integer.MAX_VALUE) {
                    firstUpper[idx] = i;
                }
            }
        }

        int count = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < 26; i++) {
            if (firstLower[i] != Integer.MAX_VALUE && firstUpper[i] != Integer.MAX_VALUE && firstLower[i] < firstUpper[i]) {
                count++;
            }
        }

        return count;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution efficiently tracks the first occurrence of each lowercase and uppercase letter. It then checks for each letter if all lowercase occurrences appear before the first uppercase occurrence. The algorithm runs in linear time and uses constant space.
